---
page-layout: full
about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: jolla
  image: assets/profile.png
  links:
    - icon: twitter
      text: Twitter
      href: https://twitter.com/LukasAd85691603
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/lukasadam
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://linkedin.com/in/lukas-adam
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: "mailto:gm.lukas.adam@gmail.com"   
format:
  html:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <link href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.0.0-beta3/css/all.min.css" rel="stylesheet">
listing:
  id: sample-listings
  contents: posts
  type: grid
  sort: "date desc"
  categories: true
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: false
  grid-columns: 3
---

```{=html}
<div class="polygon-outer"></div>
<div class="polygon-inner"></div>
<div><h1 class="polygon-header center">Hey there, I'm Lukas 👋</h1></div>
```

::: {#hero-heading}
:::

::: {.justify}
Welcome to my corner of the internet! I'm a a PhD student shared between the [Department of Biosystems Science and Engineering](https://bsse.ethz.ch/) at ETH and the [Roche Institute of Human Biology](https://institutehumanbiology.com/) (IHB) in Basel. For my PhD, I aim to resolve how gene regulatory networks are wired in the intestine and which components are truly unique to humans. To bring these mysteries to light, I’m using single-cell multi-omics, perturbation & comparative genomics data.

Beyond research, I’m passionate about coding, data exploration, and sharing knowledge. This blog is my way of combining those interests — an evolving space to teach and learn about all things data analysis, from biology to the broader world. 

Thanks for stopping by and let’s explore data related concepts together! 🚀
:::

<br><hr>

# Here are my latest blog posts {.center}

::: {#sample-listings}
:::
